source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from a VTOL tiltrotor first-responder UAS airframe architecture (center plate + arms + motor mounts + landing gear + payload housing) consistent with the NIST First Responder UAS Endurance Challenge class. No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the primary airframe for a VTOL fixed-wing UAS per the NIST First Responder UAS Endurance Challenge (HeroX) as a multi-part bolted/bonded assembly. Mission: carry a 10 lb (4.5 kg) comms-relay payload continuously for >= 90 min at 30 m/s cruise; VTOL takeoff and landing; ignition-kill capability required. Vehicle is a quad-tiltrotor tail-sitter with a 3.0 m wingspan and 20 kg MTOW (15.5 kg empty + 4.5 kg payload). The airframe subject to this prompt is the center carry-through structure and houses: a top center plate + bottom center plate sandwich assembly, four radial arms to the four tilt-rotor motor stations, four motor mount pads, landing-gear struts, a sensor-payload housing, a battery compartment, and the antenna mast. Geometric constraints: spar length 1200 mm (fuselage-width + wing-root joint both sides); spar cross-section hollow rectangular 80 mm x 40 mm with 4 mm wall; 8x M6 tapped wing-attach holes on each end in a 60 mm x 30 mm pattern; 4x M8 fuselage-attach holes at center in a 100 mm x 40 mm pattern. Material: T700S carbon fiber / epoxy unidirectional prepreg [0/+45/-45/90]s quasi-isotropic layup, E1 (fiber) = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, G12 = 5 GPa, X_tension = 2050 MPa, X_compression = 1470 MPa, Y_tension = 70 MPa, Y_compression = 170 MPa, density = 1600 kg/m^3. Load cases: (R1) 3.8 g pull-up maneuver (FAA Part 23 utility-class limit): 20 kg x 3.8 x 9.81 = 745 N lift; distributed over 3.0 m span produces wing-root bending moment of approximately 745 N x 0.75 m = 559 N*m; evaluate spar with Tsai-Wu failure index <= 1.0 / 1.5 = 0.667 (ultimate factor 1.5 per FAR 23.303). (R2) -1.5 g negative-g push-over: wing-root moment reversed, magnitude 220 N*m, Tsai-Wu index <= 0.667. (R3) 6 g hard landing: VTOL hard set-down 20 kg x 6 g = 1177 N at fuselage attach through spar, Tsai-Wu index <= 0.9 (yield factor). (R4) Tip strike during VTOL transition: 200 N horizontal at one wingtip, root moment 200 x 1.5 = 300 N*m, Tsai-Wu index <= 0.667. (R5) Spar mass <= 450 g. (R6) First modal frequency spar hard-mounted at fuselage attach with wing-tip masses lumped at ends >= 15 Hz (above UAS propeller 1P at approximately 5 Hz with 3x margin). (R7) Linear buckling eigenvalue of spar under compressive bending flange under LC1 >= 2.0. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x4 with progressive composite failure) + modal + linear buckling. Vibration secondary. Thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid spar, layup diagram, and a report listing spar mass in g, Tsai-Wu index per case, first modal frequency in Hz, first buckling load factor.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The airframe is decomposed into: (A) top center carry-through plate (hosting 4x M8 fuselage interface); (B) bottom center carry-through plate (forming sandwich with top plate around the 80 x 40 hollow spar core); (C) four radial tilt-rotor arms to the 4 motor stations; (D) four motor mount pads; (E) four landing-gear struts (VTOL tail-sitter feet); (F) one sensor/payload housing centered in the fuselage for the 4.5 kg comms relay; (G) one battery compartment forward of the payload; (H) one antenna mast on the top plate; (I) fastener set (CFRP bonded + titanium Hi-Lok fasteners at bolted interfaces). Interfaces: IF1 top plate to spar core bonded; IF2 bottom plate to spar core bonded; IF3 arms bolted through top/bottom plates (4x M6 PCD 60 x 30); IF4 motor pads bolted to arm tips; IF5 landing-gear struts bolted to bottom plate; IF6 payload housing bolted to bottom plate; IF7 battery compartment bolted to bottom plate; IF8 antenna mast bolted to top plate. FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED multi-body model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection DCR must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC3 VTOL 6 g hard landing). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the primary airframe for a VTOL fixed-wing UAS per the NIST First Responder UAS Endurance Challenge (HeroX) as a multi-part assembly. Mission: carry a 4.5 kg comms-relay payload >= 90 min at 30 m/s cruise. Quad-tiltrotor tail-sitter, 3.0 m wingspan, 20 kg MTOW.

Envelope: spar length 1200 mm, cross-section 80 mm x 40 mm hollow with 4 mm wall; 8x M6 wing-attach on 60 x 30 pattern; 4x M8 fuselage-attach on 100 x 40 pattern. Material: T700S carbon/epoxy [0/+45/-45/90]s.

Load cases: (R1) 3.8 g pull-up, wing-root moment 559 N*m, Tsai-Wu <= 0.667 (ultimate factor 1.5 per FAR 23.303). (R2) -1.5 g push-over, root moment 220 N*m, Tsai-Wu <= 0.667. (R3) 6 g hard landing, 1177 N fuselage attach, Tsai-Wu <= 0.9. (R4) Tip strike 200 N, root moment 300 N*m, Tsai-Wu <= 0.667. (R5) Spar mass <= 450 g. (R6) First modal >= 15 Hz. (R7) Buckling eigenvalue under LC1 >= 2.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Unidirectional CFRP quasi-isotropic layup requires Tsai-Wu progressive failure, not isotropic von Mises.

prompt.title: NIST First Responder UAS airframe (multi-part VTOL tiltrotor assembly)
prompt.brief: T700S quasi-iso CFRP VTOL UAS center airframe: top + bottom center plates sandwiching the 80 x 40 carry-through spar, 4 tilt-rotor arms, 4 motor pads, 4 landing-gear struts, sensor-payload housing, battery compartment, antenna mast, and fastener set. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under LC3 governing 6 g hard landing.

geometric_constraints.wing_attach_bolts: 8 x M6 on 60 x 30 pattern each end
geometric_constraints.fuselage_bolts: 4 x M8 on 100 x 40 pattern at center

boundary_conditions.mounting: fuselage-attach bolts fully fixed for structural and buckling; lumped wing-tip masses for modal
boundary_conditions.loads: root bending moments, tip forces, or VTOL reaction through landing-gear struts

parts.name: top_center_plate
parts.role: upper carry-through plate hosting fuselage interface (4x M8) and antenna mast

parts.name: bottom_center_plate
parts.role: lower carry-through plate hosting landing-gear struts, payload housing, and battery compartment

parts.name: tilt_rotor_arm
parts.role: radial arm carrying one tiltrotor at each of 4 motor stations

geometric_constraints.cross_section: hollow rectangle 40 x 30 x 3 mm wall

parts.name: motor_mount_pad
parts.role: motor mount pad at each arm tip, 4x M6 PCD 30 for tiltrotor motor

parts.name: landing_gear_strut
parts.role: VTOL tail-sitter landing-gear strut beneath the airframe (4 x legs)

parts.name: sensor_payload_housing
parts.role: housing for the 4.5 kg comms-relay payload centered in the fuselage

parts.name: battery_compartment
parts.role: battery bay forward of the payload housing for 90-min endurance battery

parts.name: antenna_mast
parts.role: comms antenna mast mounted on top center plate

parts.name: fastener_set
parts.role: titanium Hi-Lok + M6/M8 bolts at bolted interfaces; structural adhesive at bonded joints

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 3.0 m wingspan; 1200 mm fuselage carry-through; landing-gear span ~600 mm
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +X forward, +Y right wing, +Z up (VTOL tail-sitter orientation for cruise)

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC3 VTOL 6 g hard landing).

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: layered shell (composite) for plates and arms; solid for fittings
analysis_types: linear static x4 with composite failure

interfaces.load_path: top/bottom plate sandwich around the 80 x 40 carry-through spar core
tilt-rotor arm root through top/bottom plate sandwich
tiltrotor thrust + tip-strike load from motor pad into arm tip
VTOL hard-landing reaction from landing-gear struts into bottom plate
payload inertial load into bottom plate
battery inertial load into bottom plate
carry-through assembly reacts wing-root bending into fuselage

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. The VTOL 6 g hard-landing reaction crosses strut-to-plate and plate-to-spar-core joints, and tip-strike reactions cross arm-root joints, so assembly-level DCR is required; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: VTOL tiltrotor first-responder UAS airframes in this class are assembled from a CFRP top/bottom center-plate sandwich around a carry-through spar, bolted radial arms with motor pads, separate landing-gear struts, and bolted payload/battery/antenna housings rather than a single monolithic body; the VTOL landing and tip-strike loads cross multiple bolted/bonded joints.
notes.speculation_caveats: Arm count (4 tiltrotors), landing-gear strut count (4 feet for a tail-sitter), and payload/battery/antenna bracket inclusion are programmatic speculation consistent with the NIST challenge vehicle class; spar geometry, material, loads, and pass/fail thresholds inherit the original brief verbatim.
